key_highlights: Global bond selloff intensifies with US 30-year yields hitting 2007 highs, driven by rising oil prices and inflation fears.
US dollar rallies toward best week since March on Fed hike expectations, pressuring equities and emerging markets.
Cerebras IPO soars 90% in year's biggest IPO, signaling strong AI chip demand despite broader tech selloff.
Trump's China trip yields limited progress on Boeing deal (200 vs 500 planes) and Iran tensions, fueling market uncertainty.
Bond vigilantes return as JPMorgan's Kay Herr warns of sustained yield pressure, with G-7 discussing the selloff.
Active investing favored over passive as UBS notes market dispersion and volatility create opportunities.
Oil prices rise on Iran supply disruption fears, adding to stagflationary concerns.
McDonald's CEO highlights worsening K-shaped economy, with consumer anxiety rising.
Kevin Warsh appointed Fed chair, markets assess policy direction amid inflation jitters.
AI and GPU-as-a-service theme remains strong with CoreWeave, Vertiv, and Duos Technologies gaining traction.

sectors.trend: Global bond selloff deepens with US 30-year yields reaching 2007 highs, UK gilts slumping, and emerging market borrowing costs soaring. Rising oil prices and inflation fears are key drivers. G-7 discusses the selloff, and bond vigilantes are back.
sectors.implications: Higher yields pressure equity valuations, increase borrowing costs for governments and corporates, and may slow economic growth. Investors are rotating into credit for yield, but spread widening risk remains.

sectors.trend: Traders dump chipmakers as rising yields drive US stock selloff, but AI-related names like Nvidia, Cerebras, and GPU-as-a-service stocks (Vertiv, CoreWeave, Duos Technologies) remain strong. Cerebras IPO soars 90%.
sectors.implications: Broad tech selloff due to rate sensitivity, but AI infrastructure spending continues to drive demand for chips and data center services. Selective opportunities in AI beneficiaries.

sectors.trend: Oil rises on Iran supply disruption fears (Hormuz tensions, possible spill), Trump's China trip yields limited progress. Rising oil prices add to inflation concerns and bond selloff.
sectors.implications: Higher oil prices boost energy sector earnings but hurt consumers and increase inflation. Stagflation risk rises. Energy stocks may outperform in the short term.

sectors.trend: Honda retreats to hybrids after failed EV bet triggers record $9 billion loss. BYD in talks to buy idle European plants. Volkswagen brings ID. Buzz back but ID.4 on hiatus. Chinese EVs dominate globally except US.
sectors.implications: EV transition faces headwinds; hybrids gain traction. Chinese EV makers expand globally but face US barriers. Traditional automakers struggle with EV profitability.

sectors.trend: Boeing gets reduced China deal (200 vs 500 planes). Northrop Grumman and defense stocks remain in focus. Top defense stock to buy in May.
sectors.implications: Geopolitical tensions support defense spending. Boeing's China deal is positive but less than expected. Defense sector offers defensive growth.

sectors.trend: McDonald's CEO highlights worsening K-shaped economy. Subscription fatigue alienating consumers. Planet Fitness Q1 earnings call. Tax refunds lift retail sales but inflation looms.
sectors.implications: Consumer spending bifurcated; low-end consumers struggle. Subscription models face pushback. Inflation erodes purchasing power.

sectors.trend: JPMorgan's bet on early-stage companies pays off in tech investment banking. Bank of New York Mellon analyst views mixed. Private credit trading ramps up at JPMorgan.
sectors.implications: Investment banking revenue benefits from AI/tech IPO boom. Private credit market grows. Higher yields may pressure bank balance sheets but benefit net interest margins.

sectors.trend: Zillow CEO prepares for next revolution in real estate. New York plans tax on cash purchases of homes over $1 million. Mortgage rates little changed despite inflation data.
sectors.implications: Housing market faces affordability challenges. Cash purchase tax may cool luxury market. Mortgage rates remain elevated, dampening demand.

sectors.trend: Emerging markets join global selloff as borrowing costs soar. China can 'no longer be stopped'. Venezuela starts debt restructuring. Mexico's Uber crackdown complicates World Cup plans.
sectors.implications: Higher US yields and strong dollar pressure EM assets. China's growth narrative remains strong but faces headwinds. EM debt vulnerable.

sectors.trend: Bitcoin hits $82,000 as Clarity act advances. Coinbase leads crypto stock gains. Kraken cuts 150 workers after deploying AI; IPO may slip to 2027.
sectors.implications: Regulatory clarity boosts crypto markets. AI adoption in crypto firms may improve efficiency but also reduce headcount. Bitcoin rally may continue if regulatory environment improves.

key_themes: Global bond selloff and rising yields dominate markets, driven by inflation fears and oil price spikes.
AI and GPU-as-a-service remain strong investment themes despite broader tech weakness.
Geopolitical risks (Iran, China-US tensions, Middle East) add to market uncertainty.
K-shaped economy and consumer bifurcation create divergent opportunities.
Active investing favored over passive as market dispersion increases.
Fed transition to Kevin Warsh introduces policy uncertainty.
Energy transition faces headwinds; hybrids gain over pure EVs.
Private credit and alternative assets gain traction as government bonds sour.
Regulatory developments in crypto and AI compliance lag behind innovation.
Corporate restructuring and job cuts (Cisco, Kraken) reflect AI-driven efficiency.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration exposure, increase allocation to floating-rate bonds or short-term Treasuries. Consider credit for yield but monitor spreads.
Allocate to inflation-protected securities (TIPS), commodities, and real assets. Overweight energy and materials. Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary.
Increase exposure to defense stocks, energy, and gold. Hedge currency risk. Monitor diplomatic developments.
Prepare for potential hawkish shift. Reduce rate-sensitive positions. Monitor Fed communications closely.
Focus on defensive consumer staples and discount retailers. Avoid high-end discretionary. Monitor consumer credit data.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Tech Valuation Compression from Rising Yields
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to high-duration tech stocks. Focus on AI beneficiaries with strong cash flows. Consider value rotation.
Reduce EM bond exposure. Focus on hard-currency EM debt. Monitor currency risk.
Overweight energy stocks and commodities. Hedge with oil futures or ETFs. Reduce exposure to airlines and transport.
Focus on high-quality investment grade. Avoid high-yield and leveraged loans. Monitor liquidity conditions.
Monitor regulatory developments. Invest in companies with strong compliance frameworks. Avoid overvalued AI hype stocks.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase allocation to AI and GPU-as-a-service stocks (NVDA, VRT, CRWV, APLD, DUOT) on pullbacks.
opportunities.rationale: Strong secular demand for AI infrastructure, Cerebras IPO success validates theme, and corporate spending on AI continues despite broader tech weakness.

opportunities.recommendation: Overweight energy and commodities (XLE, OIH, DBC) to hedge inflation and geopolitical risks.
opportunities.rationale: Rising oil prices due to Iran tensions and supply disruptions, plus inflation fears support commodity prices. Energy sector offers strong cash flows.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in defense stocks (NOC, LMT, GD) as geopolitical tensions persist.
opportunities.rationale: Increased defense spending globally, Boeing China deal uncertainty, and Middle East tensions support defense sector growth.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider active management and factor-based strategies to capitalize on market dispersion.
opportunities.rationale: UBS notes markets favor active investing as dispersion increases. Rising yields and sector rotation create opportunities for skilled stock pickers.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy high-quality credit (IG corporate bonds) for yield as government bonds sour.
opportunities.rationale: Yield-hungry investors are rotating into credit. Spreads remain manageable, and IG offers attractive risk-adjusted returns relative to Treasuries.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in hybrid and traditional automakers (TM, HMC) as EV transition faces headwinds.
opportunities.rationale: Honda's retreat to hybrids and record loss on EVs, plus BYD's European expansion, suggest hybrids are the near-term winner. Toyota's hybrid leadership is a plus.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider crypto exposure (BTC, COIN) on regulatory clarity from Clarity act.
opportunities.rationale: Bitcoin hits $82,000 as regulatory environment improves. Coinbase leads crypto stock gains. Long-term adoption trend intact.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce duration in bond portfolios; shift to short-term Treasuries or floating-rate notes.
defensive_moves.rationale: Rising yields and bond selloff likely to continue. Short-duration instruments protect against price declines and offer reinvestment flexibility.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge equity portfolios with put options or increase allocation to defensive sectors (utilities, healthcare, consumer staples).
defensive_moves.rationale: Rising yields and inflation fears pressure equities. Defensive sectors offer relative stability. Utilities like WEC and DTE provide income.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash allocation to 10-15% to take advantage of potential market dislocations.
defensive_moves.rationale: Market volatility is elevated with bond selloff, geopolitical risks, and Fed transition. Cash provides optionality to buy on dips.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to high-beta tech and growth stocks; rotate to value and quality.
defensive_moves.rationale: Rising yields compress valuations for high-duration stocks. Value and quality factors historically outperform in rising rate environments.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge currency risk from USD strength, especially in EM and euro exposures.
defensive_moves.rationale: Dollar rallies on Fed hike view, pressuring EM currencies and euro. Hedging protects international portfolios.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Monitor and reduce exposure to consumer discretionary and subscription-based companies.
defensive_moves.rationale: Subscription fatigue and K-shaped economy hurt consumer-facing businesses. McDonald's CEO highlights worsening conditions.

market_outlook.short_term: The market faces significant headwinds from the global bond selloff, rising yields, and inflation fears. The US dollar rally and geopolitical tensions (Iran, China-US) add to uncertainty. Expect continued volatility with a defensive tone. The S&P 500 may test recent lows if yields continue to rise. Energy and defense sectors may outperform. Active management is favored. The Fed transition to Kevin Warsh introduces policy uncertainty, likely keeping markets on edge. Oil prices will be a key driver.
market_outlook.long_term: Over 6-12 months, the AI and GPU-as-a-service theme remains a structural growth driver despite near-term tech weakness. The bond selloff may stabilize if inflation moderates, but structural factors (fiscal deficits, energy transition costs) keep yields elevated. The K-shaped economy will persist, favoring high-end consumers and discount retailers. Geopolitical risks (Iran, China) will continue to support defense and energy. Crypto adoption may accelerate with regulatory clarity. Emerging markets face headwinds from strong dollar and high yields. Overall, a cautious but selective approach is warranted, with opportunities in AI infrastructure, energy, defense, and credit.
key_catalysts: Kevin Warsh's first Fed meeting and policy signals
Iran nuclear deal or escalation developments
Oil price trajectory and OPEC+ decisions
Q2 earnings season and corporate guidance
Inflation data (CPI, PCE) and labor market reports
G-7 discussions on bond market stability
Nvidia earnings and AI spending outlook
Cerebras and other AI IPO performance
World Cup preparations and tourism impact
US housing market data and mortgage rates
watch_list: Bitcoin price and crypto regulatory news
